stories.title: HMRC Issues Warning to Taxpayers Over Looming 31 July Deadline
stories.summary: HMRC has shared a reminder that the second Payments on Account deadline for the 2025 to 2026 tax year falls on Friday, 31 July 2026. Taxpayers who miss the deadline may face penalties and interest charges.

stories.title: Immigration and Asylum Bill Introduces 'Earned Settlement' to Replace 5-Year ILR Route
stories.summary: The Home Office's Immigration and Asylum Bill, introduced in the House of Commons on 30 June, proposes ending the traditional 5-year route to Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR), replacing it with an 'earned settlement' model. The changes would significantly overhaul how migrants qualify for long-term residency.

stories.title: Home Affairs Committee Examines Border Security and Irregular Migration in Northern Ireland
stories.summary: Parliament's Home Affairs Committee is scrutinising public disorder and irregular migration in Northern Ireland this week, as part of ongoing oversight of the Immigration and Asylum Bill. The session includes focus on border security and asylum procedures.

stories.title: UK Visitor Visas Now Issued Electronically as eVisas
stories.summary: UK visitor visas are now issued electronically as a secure online record, marking a significant shift in the Home Office's visa processing. The change affects all applicants for UK visitor visas going forward.

stories.title: Russia-UK Diplomatic Rift Deepens Amid Ukraine War
stories.summary: Moscow has intensified its war of words with Britain as the Russia-UK confrontation deepens amid the war in Ukraine, sanctions, and military support for Kyiv. Diplomatic tensions are at their highest levels in years.

stories.title: FCDO Updates Travel Advice for Multiple Countries Including Colombia and Indonesia
stories.summary: The FCDO has updated travel advice for several countries, including maintaining its advisory against all travel to Venezuela's border regions with Colombia, and advising against travel to several active volcanoes in Indonesia. Travellers to France, Spain, and Cyprus are also advised to check the latest guidance.

stories.title: UK Parliamentarians Question Ministers on International Law and Military Conflicts
stories.summary: Peers in the House of Lords are questioning Foreign Office ministers on international law and military conflicts as the Foreign Affairs Committee meets this week. The session reflects ongoing scrutiny of the UK's international commitments.

stories.title: DWP Confirms End of ESA and Housing Benefit as Final UC Migration Phase Completes
stories.summary: The Department for Work and Pensions has confirmed the end of income-related Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) and Housing Benefit as the final phase of migration to Universal Credit completes. Claimants on these legacy benefits have now been moved across to UC.

stories.title: Supported Housing Residents to Keep More of Their Earnings Under New Rules
stories.summary: New Housing Benefit (Earned Income Disregards) Regulations 2026, laid before parliament on 6 July 2026, will allow supported housing residents to keep more of what they earn. Five new earned income disregards come into force on 5 October 2026.

stories.title: DEFRA Confirms 50% of Sustainable Farming Incentive 2026 Budget Already Allocated
stories.summary: DEFRA has confirmed that around 50% of the Window 1 Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI) 2026 budget has now been allocated based on applications received so far. Farmers are encouraged to apply before funds run out.

stories.title: England's Farming Roadmap 2050 Proposes Shift From Animal Farming to Sustainable Crops
stories.summary: England's Farming Roadmap 2050 proposes supporting UK farmers to transition from high-emitting animal agriculture to sustainable crops such as lentils. The roadmap is generating significant discussion among farming communities about the future of English agriculture.

stories.title: Sanction Authorised for MoD After Two Soldiers Died in Challenger 2 Tank Explosion
stories.summary: A sanction has been authorised for the Ministry of Defence following the deaths of Cpl Darren Neilson and Cpl Matthew Hatfield, who died during an explosion on a Challenger 2 tank at Castlemartin Range. The MoD faces accountability measures following the incident.

stories.title: Government Launches Call for Evidence on AI-Enabled Toy Safety
stories.summary: The government has stepped up action to protect children as AI-enabled toys emerge, launching a Call for Evidence to ensure toy safety rules are fit for the modern age. The review will examine emerging risks including chemical safety, AI interactions, and data privacy for children.

stories.title: UK Inflation Holds Steady at 2.8% in May 2026
stories.summary: UK inflation remained at 2.8% in the year to May 2026, according to the Office for National Statistics — unchanged from the previous month. The figure reflects ongoing cost pressures in key sectors including food and services.

stories.title: DSIT Publishes Findings from 'Future Tech Sector That Works for Everyone' Call for Evidence
stories.summary: DSIT has released findings from its call for evidence on building a future tech sector that works for everyone, launched in March 2026. The report examines barriers faced by under-represented groups in the UK technology industry.

stories.title: New Research Quantifies Economic Cost of Digital Economy Inequality
stories.summary: DSIT-commissioned research by Alma Economics has quantified the economic costs of under-representation of certain groups in the UK digital economy. The findings support the Women in Tech Taskforce launched in December 2025 by Secretary of State Liz Kendall.

stories.title: NHS to Rate Trusts on How Well They Tackle Racism, Violence and Sexual Misconduct Against Staff
stories.summary: From July 2026, all NHS trusts in England will be rated according to how well they tackle racism, violence, and sexual misconduct towards staff. The government-backed initiative aims to improve staff wellbeing and reduce workplace harm across the health service.

stories.title: Health Secretary Seeks Advice Over Maternity Inquiry Staff Who Refused to Participate
stories.summary: Health Secretary James Murray has described it as 'totally unacceptable' that some senior staff refused to take part in the inquiry into maternity services at Nottingham University Hospitals. He is seeking legal advice on potential consequences for those who declined to cooperate.

stories.title: NHS England Plans to Give Patients Three Weeks' Notice Before Hospital Treatment
stories.summary: NHS England is planning to give people awaiting hospital treatment at least three weeks' notice of their appointment, to improve efficiency and reduce no-shows. The change is intended to help hospitals better manage their waiting lists.

stories.title: Boss of Royal Mail Owner IDS Sees Pay More Than Triple to Nearly £7m
stories.summary: The chief executive of International Distributions Services (IDS), owner of Royal Mail, has seen their pay more than triple to nearly £7 million. However, Royal Mail's UK postal arm saw annual operating profits more than halve after labour costs soared following minimum wage hikes and additional costs of £133 million.

stories.title: CWU Union Tensions Over Royal Mail Restructuring Under Kretinsky Ownership
stories.summary: The Communication Workers Union (CWU) is facing criticism over its approach to Royal Mail's restructuring under owner Daniel Kretinsky, with claims the union lacks a mandate for its stance. The dispute centres on plans described as 'full optimisation' that workers say aim to eliminate jobs and intensify workloads.
